articles.title: Uzbekistan puts former migration official on Interpol Red Notice
articles.summary_raw: Uzbekistan has placed a former migration agency official on Interpol’s Red Notice list over fraud allegations linked to a labor migration corruption case involving South Korea.

articles.title: In pictures: Hyundai Rotem train makes inaugural Tashkent-Khiva journey
articles.summary_raw: The new high-speed Hyundai Rotem train, named Jaloliddin Manguberdi, made its inaugural Tashkent-Khiva journey. The 1,022 km trip takes 7.5 hours — nearly twice as fast as a conventional train. Uzbekistan became the first country in the region and the CIS to buy the South Korea-made trains.

articles.title: Jackie Chan film in Kazakhstan eyes $50M budget
articles.summary_raw: The chairman of the Respublica political party, Aidarbek Khojanazarov, on the sidelines of the Mazhilis, shared details about the upcoming filming of Jackie Chan’s «Armour of God,» which will take place in Kazakhstan. He said the film will cost $50 million. Part of the budget, Khojanazarov said, will go toward salaries for the Kazakhstani film ...

articles.title: President of Uzbekistan posthumously awards British couple for saving Uzbek soldier during World War II
articles.summary_raw: President of Uzbekistan posthumously awarded the Dostlik (Friendship) Order to the British couple John and Phyliss Emily Le Breton for saving the life of Uzbek soldier Bokijon Akramov during World War II.

Kazakhstan reports sharp rise in gold reserves
articles.summary_raw: Kazakhstan’s gross international reserves reached $67.78 billion, up 3.62% since the beginning of 2026, according to preliminary data from the National Bank as of May 1. Monetary gold emerged as the main driver again. Its volume over the first months of 2026 increased by 11.55% to $52.69 billion. At the same time, assets in convertible ...

articles.title: Kazakhstan prepares to test its first air taxi
articles.summary_raw: Kazakhstani company Alatau Advance Air Group is going to test its first air taxi, based on electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) technology, at the end of May. Alatau: A special environment for the development of Urban Air Mobility On May 8, President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev signed a law granting special status to the city of Alatau. ...

articles.title: President Erdogan visits Kazakhstan, hails country’s warm welcome
articles.summary_raw: During Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan‘s state visit to Astana, Kazakhstan’s President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev warmly welcomed the Turkish leader and thanked him for accepting the invitation. He emphasized that Erdogan’s visit is of exceptional significance for Kazakhstan and described it as a historic event. Erdogan said the Turkish delegation was impressed by Kazakh Air Force ...

articles.title: Astana hosts $1.6 million Counter-Strike 2 tournament
articles.summary_raw: Kazakhstan’s capital, Astana, will host a top-tier international Counter-Strike 2 tournament, PGL Astana 2026, from May 15 to 17. This will mark the second time Kazakhstan has hosted one of the world’s largest esports events, the Ministry of Tourism and Sports reported. PGL Esports SRL is a tournament organizer; the total prize pool is $1.6 ...

articles.title: Sovereignty in the stars: Kazakhstan’s security rules for Starlink
articles.summary_raw: SpaceX subsidiary Starlink, which already provides internet access in Kazakhstan through low-Earth-orbit satellites, will be required to register in the country and purchase internet traffic from local operators, according to Zhaslan Madiyev, Kazakhstan’s deputy prime minister and minister of AI and digital development. «Satellite internet service is supplementary to internet provided via fiber-optic networks. The ...

articles.title: Chinese firm weighs $12 billion aluminum investment in Kazakhstan
articles.summary_raw: Chinese East Hope Group — one of the world’s largest aluminum producers — could invest $12.6 billion in bauxite mining and aluminum production in Kazakhstan, according to Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Industry and Construction. «Minister of Industry and Construction Yersayin Nagaspayev held talks with East Hope Group Director for Strategic Investments Chen Lei…The company is conducting ...

articles.title: Kazakhstan develops homegrown drug for mugwort pollen allergy
articles.summary_raw: Scientists at the Kazakh National Agrarian Research University have begun the second phase of clinical trials for a drug to treat mugwort pollen allergy, the Ministry of Agriculture of Kazakhstan reported. According to the ministry, the PollenVax immunotherapy drug, developed under the Ministry of Healthcare program, has successfully completed the first phase of trials assessing ...

articles.title: Survival of the fittest? Kazakhstan blames natural selection for mass deaths
articles.summary_raw: Kazakhstan’s government has responded to growing concerns over the condition of the country’s wildlife, addressing not only the recent saiga die-off in the steppes but also the deaths of swans and Caspian seals in western Kazakhstan. Nurken Sharbiyev, Kazakhstan’s deputy minister of ecology, said the recorded deaths of 26,000 saigas — including 11,000 in the ...

articles.title: Water security: Why Kyrgyzstan’s new mega-dam matters to Kazakhstan
articles.summary_raw: Kazakhstan, together with Uzbekistan and Kyrgyzstan, plans to move into the practical construction phase of the Kambarata-1 Hydropower Plant project, according to Jamshid Khodjaev, Uzbekistan’s deputy prime minister. Read also: Kazakhstan to fund massive Kyrgyz dam for energy security. Khodjaev said the 1,860-megawatt Kambarata-1 HPP is expected to become the largest power plant in Central ...

articles.title: Eye in the sky: Kazakhstan rolls out China-style behavior tracking
articles.summary_raw: Kazakhstan is rolling out a nationwide video surveillance system powered by artificial intelligence that will be used to detect aggressive behavior, fights and disorderly conduct. The initiative was announced by Aida Balayeva, Kazakhstan’s minister of culture and information, in response to a request from Kudaibergen Beksultanov, a member of Parliament. According to Balayeva, more than ...

articles.title: Loan boom: Kazakh banks expand as authorities seek a cooldown
articles.summary_raw: Kazakhstan’s banking statistics for March reflected a 3.8% strengthening of the tenge. The sector also benefited from increased liquidity driven by an inflow of corporate deposits, allowing banks to partially restore assets to levels seen at the beginning of 2026. The loan portfolio fully recovered, although growth continued to be driven primarily by retail lending. ...

articles.title: A new wave: Why Russian-speakers in Kazakhstan are embracing the native tongue
articles.summary_raw: While Russian politicians and commentators speak of the alleged «oppression of Russians» in Kazakhstan, some Russian speakers in the country are helping popularize the Kazakh language. Through music, Instagram and language clubs, Kazakh is becoming the new norm for many of them, Deutsche Welle reported in a recent article. A shifting linguistic landscape For many ...

articles.title: Uzbekistan to raise limit for foreign cash currency purchases without ID to $500
articles.summary_raw: Uzbekistan will raise the limit for foreign cash currency purchases without an ID from $100 to $500. The new regulations will come into effect on 9 August.

articles.title: No hantavirus cases recorded in Uzbekistan — Sanitary-Epidemiological Committee
articles.summary_raw: No cases of hantavirus were recorded in Uzbekistan, and the epidemiological situation remains stable, first deputy chairman of the Sanitary-Epidemiological Committee Nurmat Atabekov said. He added that the WHO does not consider the virus capable of causing a pandemic.

articles.title: Sweden deports 20 citizens of Uzbekistan
articles.summary_raw: Sweden deported 20 citizens of Uzbekistan for violating migration laws. The group arrived in Tashkent on a charter flight.

articles.title: Exchange of blows: how Sindarov and Gukesh’s first encounter unfolded ahead of title match
articles.summary_raw: Uzbekistan’s chess player Javokhir Sindarov tied for 7th-9th at the Super Rapid & Blitz Poland in Warsaw, with attention focused on his games against reigning World Champion Dommaraju Gukesh. Indian grossmeister won the rapid, while Sindarov responded with two blitz wins ahead of their title match.

articles.title: Uzbekistan to restructure Health Ministry with new bodies and expanded powers
articles.summary_raw: By presidential decree, Uzbekistan’s Health Ministry will establish three new bodies, including a research center, a clinical audit inspectorate and a media center, while advancing digitalization. The reforms were announced following a major reshuffle in the ministry’s leadership.

articles.title: Current times require greater appreciation of peace — president of Uzbekistan
articles.summary_raw: President of Uzbekistan said that current times require greater appreciation of peace and stability in society, as well as friendship and harmony among different nations and faiths. On the Day of Memory and Honor, he congratulated “fraternal peoples and all people of goodwill”.

articles.title: In pictures: President of Uzbekistan at Victory Day Parade in Moscow
articles.summary_raw: President of Uzbekistan took part in events in Moscow marking the 81st anniversary of Victory in World War II. Alongside the president of Russia, leaders of Belarus, Kazakhstan, Laos, Malaysia and other countries attended the Victory Day Parade.

articles.title: Abdulla Abdukadirov appointed acting head of reforms agency
articles.summary_raw: Abdulla Abdukadirov has been appointed acting director of Uzbekistan’s Agency for Strategic Development and Reforms under a presidential decree issued on 12 May.

articles.title: Uzbekistan sends 100 citizens on free Hajj pilgrimage
articles.summary_raw: Uzbekistan will send 100 citizens on a free Hajj pilgrimage under the Presidential Hajj program, the Muslim Board of Uzbekistan said.

articles.title: Uzbekistan sells over 121,000 vehicles in Jan–Apr 2026
articles.summary_raw: Uzbekistan’s car market recorded 121,601 vehicle sales in January–April 2026, with UzAuto Motors maintaining a dominant market share.

articles.title: Rosatom presents integrated nuclear plant concept in Tashkent
articles.summary_raw: Rosatom showcased integrated nuclear power plant technology and e-mobility solutions at Power Uzbekistan 2026, highlighting its role in Uzbekistan’s energy development.

articles.title: OSCE and international partners discuss the future development of Central Asia at a conference in Tashkent
articles.summary_raw: An international conference titled “Central Asia: A New Paradigm for Regional Development” was held in Tashkent, organized by the Diplomatic Academy at the University of World Economy and Diplomacy (UWED) in partnership with the OSCE Project Co-ordinator in Uzbekistan.

articles.title: Tashkent Initiative Adopted at China–Central Asia Forum
articles.summary_raw: Uzbekistan hosted the China–Central Asia Human Rights Forum in Tashkent, where participants adopted the “Tashkent Initiative” on development and cooperation.

articles.title: Uzbekistan sets record in solar and wind power output
articles.summary_raw: Uzbekistan reached a new record in daily solar and wind electricity generation, producing 62.4 million kWh and increasing the share of green energy.

articles.title: Uzbekistan, Condor Energies discuss gas output growth
articles.summary_raw: Uzbekneftegaz and Canada’s Condor Energies reviewed progress on gas field development in the Gazli region and plans to increase production efficiency.

articles.title: Uzbekistan launches major energy projects with AMEA Power
articles.summary_raw: Uzbekistan has started large-scale energy projects with UAE-based AMEA Power, including wind power and battery storage systems worth over US$1.3 billion.

articles.title: Personal debt in Uzbekistan rises as experts advise budgeting
articles.summary_raw: Experts from Alpari analyze rising household debt in Uzbekistan and offer budgeting, saving, and investment strategies to improve financial stability.

articles.title: Uzbek President to visit Kazakhstan on working trip
articles.summary_raw: Uzbekistan’s president will visit Kazakhstan for a Turkic States informal summit focused on AI, digital development and regional cooperation.

articles.title: Shavkat Mirziyoyev appoints new presidential energy advisor
articles.summary_raw: Uzbekistan President Shavkat Mirziyoyev appointed Behzod Normatov as advisor on energy and fuel issues, replacing previous energy security representative.

articles.title: Uzbekistan and Kazakhstan expand oil and gas cooperation
articles.summary_raw: Uzbekistan and Kazakhstan discussed expanding cooperation in oil, gas, and petrochemicals during the OGU 2026 energy conference in Tashkent.

articles.title: Uzbekistan court awards compensation in copyright case
articles.summary_raw: A court in Uzbekistan ordered a university to pay compensation to translator Mark Reese after confirming illegal distribution of his English translation of “Days Gone By.”

articles.title: Indonesia and Russia discuss nuclear energy cooperation
articles.summary_raw: Rosatom’s Director General Alexei Likhachev has said Russia is ready to offer Indonesia a comprehensive approach to developing its national nuclear programme, ranging from gigawatt-scale units to small modular reactors and floating power plants.

articles.title: President of Uzbekistan leaves for Moscow to attend Victory Day celebrations
articles.summary_raw: President of Uzbekistan Shavkat Mirziyoyev departed on a working visit to Russia. He will take part in events in Moscow marking the 81st anniversary of Victory in the World War II, his press service said.

articles.title: UPDATED: President of Uzbekistan to attend 9 May parade in Moscow
articles.summary_raw: President of Uzbekistan Shavkat Mirziyoyev will attend the 9 May Victory Day celebrations in Moscow. Earlier, source told Gazeta and other media that the visit was not planned. The Kremlin’s published list of invited foreign leaders did not include the presidents of Central Asian countries.

articles.title: Uzbekistan plans to become full WTO member in 2026
articles.summary_raw: Uzbekistan aims to become a full member of the World Trade Organization in 2026. The issue was discussed at a meeting chaired by president. The finalization of the accession was planned for March, but was delayed.

articles.title: Sixty World War II veterans and persons of equivalent status currently live in Uzbekistan
articles.summary_raw: According to the Defense Ministry, 60 World War II veterans and persons of equivalent status currently live in Uzbekistan. This includes disabled and combat veterans, survivors of the Siege of Leningrad and former Nazi concentration camp prisoners. The largest number of veterans resides in Tashkent.

articles.title: Citizen of Türkiye extradited to Uzbekistan from Peru on fraud charges
articles.summary_raw: A citizen of Türkiye wanted by Uzbekistan via Interpol on suspicion of large-scale fraud was extradited from Peru. In a separate case, three nationals of Uzbekistan, including one woman, were extradited from the UAE. They are charged with human trafficking and fraud.

articles.title: President of Uzbekistan reshuffles Health Ministry leadership
articles.summary_raw: President of Uzbekistan appointed the new health minister and replaced almost all of his deputies. Eldor Adilov succeeded Asilbek Khudayarov as the minister. Only one deputy minister retained his post.

articles.title: President of Uzbekistan proposes to ADB expanding projects in AI, energy, minerals and logistics
articles.summary_raw: President of Uzbekistan, speaking at the ADB Board of Governors meeting, proposed to expand cooperation in AI, green energy, logistics digitalization, critical minerals, climate and tourism projects in Central Asia. He noted that global conflicts were raising transport costs by as up to 30%.

articles.title: Uzbekistan, ADB agree on $12.5 billion in projects through 2030
articles.summary_raw: President of Uzbekistan and head of the Asian Development Bank agreed on a new partnership program through 2030 worth $12.5 billion, covering infrastructure, reform support, private sector and public-private partnerships. The current project portfolio exceeds $15.5 billion.

articles.title: 26 more citizens of Uzbekistan deported from US on 36-hour flight
articles.summary_raw: US deported 26 more citizens of Uzbekistan for immigration violations. An aircraft operated by Omni Air International made five stopovers en route from Texas to Tashkent. The total travel time was 36 hours.
